blocks.text: Policies on Faculty Workload
I. Rationale
Jose Rizal Memorial State University commits to provide quality

instruction to all its students delivered by qualified and competent faculty members. With the influx of students in the University due to the free higher education, the student population has increased significantly. Hence, this impacts the faculty workload and eventually requires additional teaching force for the various program offerings across campuses.

Having operated for more than ten (10) years as a university, it has encountered a number of challenges which have been recurring and unresolved since the existing policies on faculty workload are limited in nature.

Hence, the university needs to update and align its institutional policies especially on the regular teaching load of permanent faculty members including the payment of honorarium and overtime pay for the teaching overload pursuant
to the 2024 Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) issued by the Commission on Audit.
II. Policy Statements
Section 1 In accordance with the Article 83 of the Labor Code of the
Philippines, as amended, the normal hours of work of school personnel shall not exceed eight (8) hours a day.
Any work done in addition to the eight (8) hours daily work shall constitute overtime work (CMO 40, s.2008; CMO 30, s.2009).
Section 2 Each head of the department in the SUC may allow

flexible working hours for his/her faculty members so they can perform their four-fold functions of instruction, consultation, research and extension services, provided that the prescribed forty hours of work per week shall
strictly be implemented (CSC MC No. 19, s.2005).
Section 3 Agencies in government may adopt compressed

workweek that refers to a work arrangement whereby the forty (40) hours workweek for five (5) days of the government officials or employees is compressed to four (4) days or less, as may be applicable (CSC MC No. 06, s.2022).
Section 4 The regular teaching load of full-time academic teaching
personnel shall be determined by the higher education
institution but in no case shall exceed 24 units per semester or term (CMO 40, s.2008; CM0 30, s.2009).
Section 5 Any teaching load in excess of the normal or regular

teaching load shall be considered as overload. Overload partakes of the nature of temporary extra assignment and compensation therefore shall be considered as an overload honorarium if performed within the 8-hour work period and does not form part of the regular or basic pay.
Overload performed beyond the eight-hour daily work is
overtime work (CMO 40, s.2008; CMO 30, s.2009).
Section 6 Honoraria may be paid to teaching personnel engaged in
actual classroom teaching, whose teaching load is outside of the regular office hours or in excess of the regular load
(Sec.63 (a) General Provisions of RA 11975).
Section 7 Government employees whose regular schedule of work
falls between 6 a.m. and 6 p.m., including those under the flexible work arrangements whose nature of services requires their rendition within the same period are
excluded from the grant of Night Shift Differential Pay. The
services they rendered beyond the regular eight (8) – hour work schedules shall be compensated in accordance with the existing laws, rules, and regulations on overtime
services and overtime pay (CSC-DBM-GCGOCCs Joint Circular No. 1, s.2023).
7.1 An employee whose prescribed work of schedule is from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. but renders services until 7 p.m.
as authorized by the head of agency. The hours worked from 6 p.m. to 7 p.m. shall not be covered by Night Shift
Differential Pay, but shall be compensated in accordance
with the rules and regulations an overtime services and overtime pay.

7.2 An employee whose agency implements compressed workweek with ten (10) working hours for four days in a week, has the prescribed work schedule from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. with 12 p.m. to 1 p.m. as the time for lunch.
The hours worked from 6 p.m. to 7 p.m. shall not be
covered by Night Shift Differential Pay. In case the

employee renders services beyond the prescribed ten (10) hours of work, as authorized by the head of agency, he/she shall be compensated in accordance with the rules and regulations on overtime services and overtime pay.
III. Terms Defined
Compensatory Overtime Credits (COCs) – are any

units of WL aside from actual teaching load in excess of 24 units/ term. These are pooled/ lumped into compensatory time-offs (CTOS) by the HRMO that can be used by any faculty to cover his/her absences or undertime.
Seasonal/Occasional Designations – refer to
designations which do not require daily and weekly client engagement, and monthly submissions of reportorial requirements and conduct of office-initiated activities.
Teaching Load (TL) refers to actual teaching loads given

to faculty members measured in hours load of subjects to teach (under a. instruction) assigned to a teacher. It excludes work assignments in b) research, c) extension, d) production and e) others/administrative designations.
Illustration :

| Credit Unit(s) | Teaching Hour(s) | Teaching Load (in hours) |
|---|---|---|
| 3 (Lec) | 3 | 3 |
| 2 (Lec) | 2 | 2 |
| 1 (Lec) | 1 | 1 |

| Credit Unit(s) | Teaching Hour(s) | Teaching Load (in hours), except high- risk laboratory work exposure(s) |
|---|---|---|
| 3 (Lab) | 9 | 6.25 |
| 2 (Lab) | 6 | 4.5 |
| 1 (Lab) | 3 | 2.25 |
Teaching Overload (TO) refers to actual teaching load in

excess of the 24 units that will be paid honoraria/overtime pay subject to existing policies thereof and subject further to availability of funds. Teaching overload is treated as overtime if rendered after office hours on a scheduled workday, on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day. The maximum TO that may be compensated is 10 full units equivalent to 10 teaching hours or as provided in the programs, standards, and guidelines of their respective programs. In cases where funds are not sufficient, only a percentage of TO may be paid as determined by the available fund. Any excess units not compensated due to unavailability of funds are converted to service credits.
Workload (WL) refers to loads of work given to faculty
members per term that includes assignments/ functions in a) instruction, b) research, c) extension, d) production/ IGP
and e) others/administrative designations. The regular WL of a faculty in Jose Rizal Memorial State University is 24 units.
IV. Faculty Classification and Ranking
Section 1 Academic teaching positions shall be classified in
accordance with academic qualifications, training and

scholarship preferably into Professor, Associate Professor, Assistant Professor, and Instructor, without prejudice to a more simplified or expanded system of faculty ranking, at the option of the institution (CMO 40,

An academic teaching personnel, who does not fall under any of the classes or ranks indicated in the preceding paragraph shall be classified preferably as professorial lecturer, guest lecturer, or any other similar academic designation on the basis of his qualifications (CMO 40,
Section 2 The ‘lecturer’ designation is given to a non-regular
member of the teaching staff who is paid on hourly basis.
Lecturer may be renewed on a semester basis.
Depending on their qualifications, lecturers may be given
a rank of “Lecturer”, “Senior Lecturer”, or “Professorial Lecturer” (UP Faculty Manual).
Section 3 A lecturer is a faculty member who has earned units and
is currently pursuing MA/MS degree program
aligned/allied to his/her field of specialization. A senior
lecturer is a full-fledged MA/MS degree holder
aligned/allied to his/her field of specialization whose performance and accomplishments are comparable to at least an Assistant Professor I rank. A professional lecturer
is full-fledged PhD/EdD or other doctorate degree holder
whose performance and accomplishments.
V. General Policies
Section 1 All faculty members holding permanent positions/ items
must first be given regular WL for the semester before
additional teachers (under Contract of Service) will be
hired.
Section 2 Faculty members must be informed of their tentative
teaching loads (TL) at least one (1) week before the start of classes.
Section 3 The percentage distribution of job function vis- à-vis major
thrusts of the University shall be specified in the Individual
Performance Commitment and Review (IPCR).
Section 4 A faculty member with a WL & TL in excess of 30

hours/week within office hours, after office hours on a scheduled workday, on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day shall be entitled to an honorarium/ overtime pay for the teaching overload subject to the availability of funds or is granted service credit equivalence.
Section 5 All other assignments with monetary remuneration shall
not be given any workload equivalent.
Section 6 The IGP Manual of the University shall be used as guide
when a faculty member is involved in Production function.
Section 7 A faculty member must satisfy the mandatory functions
before accepting optional loads.
Section 8 In the exigency of the service, additional loads may be
given to faculty with more than the minimum WL.
Section 9 The WL/TL required must be used as basis in rating the
performance of faculty members for Performance-Based Bonus (PBB) and Performance Enhancement Incentives
(PEI), Collective Negotiation Agreement (CNA), and other
bonuses subject to existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
VI. Faculty Workload
Section 1 A regular teaching load of at least twenty-four (24) units a
week for each faculty member shall be required, but with a maximum service of forty (40) hours a week including consultation, research, extension and quasi-teaching administrative functions.
Section 2 The teaching load of a permanent/regular faculty member
shall be determined vis- à-vis the teaching preparation as follows:
Table VI.1 Teaching Preparation

| Faculty without designation | ← |
|---|---|
| Teaching Load (in units) | No. of Teaching Preparation(s) |
| 24 units | 1-2 preparations |
| 21 units | 3-4 preparations |
| 15 units and below | 5 and above |
| Faculty with designation | ← |
| Teaching Load (in units) | No. of Teaching Preparation(s) |
| 18-21 units | not more than 3 preparations |
| 12-15 units | not more than 2 preparations |
Section 3 There shall be at least fifteen (15) students to open a
class/course in any undergraduate program in the first- year level.
Section 4 In a special case that a lecture and a laboratory course
are handled/taught by two separate instructor/professor, the lecture and laboratory courses shall be treated differently and be considered one (1) preparation.
Section 5 Faculty member(s) with non-seasonal/occasional
designations shall avail of the release hours in the performance of duties and functions attached to the administrative work.
Table VI.2 Non-Seasonal/Occasional Designation

| Designations | Release Hours | Required Teaching Load (in units) | Earning Sick/Vacation Leave Credits |
|---|---|---|---|
| Vice President | 24 | (may opt to take teaching and/or research load) | ✓ |
| Campus Director | 24 | (may opt to take teaching and/or research load) | ✓ |
| Dean (under- graduate & graduate programs) | 12 | 12 | ✓ |
| Associate Dean | 12 | 12 | ✓ |
| University Director (Academic Programs, Research & Extension, KTTO) | 12 | 12 | ✓ |

| University Director (UDRMMS, IGP, Security Services) | 9 | 15 | ✓ |
|---|---|---|---|
| Center Director, CeMarSec, RCCPCW, and the like | 9 | 15 | ✓ |
| Unit Head, Research | 6 | 15 | ✓ |
| Unit Head, Extension | 6 | 15 | ✓ |
| Unit Head, CDRMMS | 6 | 18 | ✓ |
| Unit Head, Registrar | 6 | 18 | ✓ |
| Unit Head, IGP | 6 | 18 | ✓ |
| Unit Head, NSTP | 6 | 18 | x |
| Unit Head, Quality Assurance | 6 | 18 | ✓ |
| Unit Head, Guidance Services | 6 | 18 | ✓ |
| Administrative Campus Level-BAC Chair & Members | 6 | 18 | x |
| Publication Manager (The Threshold) | 6 | 18 | x |
| FabLab Manager | 6 | 18 | x |

| Chairperson, Laboratory High School | 6 | 18 | x |
|---|---|---|---|
| Academic Program Chairpersons (3 release hours for 300 students and below, an additional 1 hour for every 100 students in the program) | 3 | 21 | x |
| Administrative Campus Level- BACTWG | 3 | 21 | x |
| Campus Laboratory In- charge | 3 | 21 | x |
| Unit Head, Security Services | 3 | 21 | ✓ |
| College Secretaries | 3 | 21 | ✓ |
| Shipboard Training Officer (STO) | 0 | 40 | ✓ |
Section 6 Faculty member(s) with seasonal/occasional designations
shall carry the regular load of 24 units in a semester.

However, they shall be entitled to an overtime pay for the overtime services rendered in the performance of administrative duties and functions, provided that is done after office hours and during weekends.
Section 7 Faculty member(s) with seasonal/occasional designations
shall make a formal request with the attached form (Authority to render overtime) duly noted by the immediate
supervisor, campus budget officer, and approved by the
Campus Director/University President prior to the
rendition of overtime services.
Section 8 The immediate supervisor, as accountable officer, shall
ascertain the number of hours rendered as indicated in the
Daily Time Record (DTR) commensurate to the output(s)
presented. Proof(s) shall no longer be attached to the DTR, and the immediate supervisor may require additional supporting documents and other objective evidences.
Table VI.3 Seasonal/Occasional Designation

| Designations | Maximum Allowable No. of Hours for Overtime (in a week) | Maximum Allowable No. of Hours for Over-time (in a semester – 18 weeks) |
|---|---|---|
| University Director (So- cio-cultural Affairs, PESA, REC, EALRG, Alumni Affairs, FLS, IMD, GAD, Sentro ng Wika at Kultura | 9 | 162 |
| University Director. Admission & Testing (except 2nd semester of the academic year) | 6 | 108 |
| Unit Head, Admission & Testing (except 2nd semester of the academic year) | 6 | 108 |
| Unit Head, IMD | 6 | 108 |
| Unit Head, FLS | 6 | 108 |
| Unit Head, GAD | 6 | 108 |
| Unit Head, REC | 6 | 108 |
| Unit Head, ITSO | 6 | 108 |

| RDE Database Manager | 6 | 108 |
|---|---|---|
| Unit Head, TBI | 6 | 108 |
| Unit Head, EALRG & Alumni Affairs | 6 | 108 |
| Chairperson, Research | 3 | 54 |
| Chairperson, Extension | 3 | 54 |
| Chairperson, IMD | 3 | 54 |
| Chairperson, GAD | 3 | 54 |
| Chairperson, QA | 3 | 54 |
| Campus & College Level-Student Affairs and Services | 3 | 54 |
| Academic Campus Level-Lead Auditors | 3 | 54 |
| Academic Campus Level-BI Liaison Officer | 3 | 54 |
Section 9 Faculty members with administrative designations

required to report to the station on semestral break and midyear break shall be entitled to earn sick/vacation leave credit(s). The faculty member assigned to the aforementioned designation shall formally request approval for the entitlement to earn sick and vacation leave credits. This request shall be duly noted by the immediate supervisor(s) and requires final approval from
the University President.
Section 10 Should the designation be revoked for reasonable
ground(s), the appointing authority shall communicate and furnish the immediate supervisor(s) and the Human
Resource Management Officer a copy of the revocation
order for the conversion of the sick/vacation credits to service credits.
Section 11 No faculty member shall be designated to a non-

seasonal/occasional administrative position within/during the regular duration of classes. The requesting party shall seek the approval of the designating authority and the concurrence of the faculty at least a month before the

(1st official start of the academic year semester). In the exigency of service, the faculty concerned shall not avail of the equivalent release hours and carry the teaching load of the current semester as given and conformed. He/she may be compensated for the overtime services rendered as required/as necessary.
Section 12 No faculty member, designated to a non-

seasonal/occasional administrative work, shall be allowed to tender resignation of the designation within/during the regular duration of classes, except for medical condition and other valid reasons. His/her underload shall be treated subject to the usual accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
Section 13 All non-seasonal/occasional administrative designations
shall be revoked only at the beginning of the academic year.
Section 14 Full-time lecturers shall be allowed to carry a maximum of
thirty (30) teaching hours per week with at least three (3) or more preparations, except for the lecturers in the
College of Nursing and Allied Health Sciences/ School of Midwifery, College of Maritime Education, College of

Industrial Technology, College of Computing Studies, and College of Engineering. The maximum number of teaching hours shall be stretched reasonably in special case(s) when the number of units and the corresponding teaching hours could not be reduced nor split.
Section 15 Part-time lecturers who wish to teach in the undergraduate
program shall be allowed to carry a maximum of 15 teaching hours per week with at least 3 or more preparations.
Section 16 A faculty shall be designated with a maximum of two (2)
designations.
Section 17 For faculty with two or more honorific designations/special

positions, a three-hour deduction from the designated teaching load for every additional designation/special position shall be effected, provided that the 25% of the official time of faculty members concerned shall be credited to actual teaching load.
Section 18 For faculty with approved research/creative
works/extension proposals, a 6-hour deduction shall be

made from the designated teaching load for every research/creative work/extension externally funded with a grant of not lower than 1 million pesos (Php 1M), provided that the deducted number of hours shall not be more than fifty (50) percent of the specified teaching hours so as not to jeopardize instruction, provided further that no faculty member shall be entitled of monetary incentive if granted with load release(s). The aforementioned release hours shall be availed until such time the project/program is officially terminated.
Section 19 For a faculty student enrolled in the doctorate degree

program aligned/allied to his/her field of specialization from a reputable institution shall avail of the 3-unit study load credit, provided that he/she has officially enrolled at least 9 units in a term.
Section 20 Student faculty member who is on dissertation writing

shall avail of the six (6) units study load credit. Faculty members whose fields of specialization are on moratorium shall not be entitled to administrative support such as, but not limited to, financial assistance, official time for dissertation-related activities, among others.
Section 21 For faculty student who is either a full-fledged master’s

degree or a doctorate degree holder already and intends to pursue another postgraduate degree from a reputable institution for the vertical alignment of his/her field of specialization shall avail of the 3-unit study load credit, except for fields of specialization on moratorium.
Section 22 Faculty members with study load credit (SLC) should not

be assigned any overload teaching. If a student faculty member is being considered for overload honorarium, the SLC shall not be counted except under an emergency situation as in the following example: a faculty member, in the middle of a semester, leaves the service (for whatever reason), and the department/college cannot find a replacement/substitute except a faculty student. In this case, the student faculty member should be compensated additionally.
Section 23 In the exigency of service, the University may opt for
assigning teaching overload to regular faculty members or hire additional qualified and competent faculty from the
administrative personnel or even from other agencies/entities with the prescribed workload as follows:
Table VI.4 Teaching Overload for Lecturers

| Human Resource | Workload |
|---|---|
| 1. faculty without administrative designation | maximum of 10 teaching hours overload or as followed by their respective PSGs in a semester across programs |
| 2. faculty with administrative designation (regardless of release hours) | maximum of 6 teaching hours overload in a semester across programs |
| 3.regular administrative personnel | maximum of 6 teaching hours in a semester across programs |
| 4. administrative support staff-COS | maximum of 15 teaching hours in a semester |
| 5. government- employed VL (part- time) | maximum of 15 teaching hours/or as allowed by the mother agency in a semester across programs |
| 6. privately employed VL (part- time) | Maximum of 15 teaching hours/or as allowed by the mother agency in a semester across programs |
Section 24 The maximum number of teaching hours for lecturers shall

likewise be stretched reasonably in special case(s) when the number of units handled and the corresponding teaching hours could not be reduced nor split (Illustration: 12 units + additional 4-unit course=16 units equivalent to 16 teaching hours).
Section 25 The following shall be compensated of the teaching
services rendered via asynchronous mode of delivery, while on official travel, provided that the conditions set have been satisfied:
25.1 (a) Regular/Permanent faculty members with

teaching overload in excess of 30 hours/week within office hours, teaching overload after office hours, or during weekends; b) Administrative personnel engaged in part-time teaching, regardless of employment status with teaching load after office hours or during weekends, and (c) Lecturers (full-time and part-time), on official travel, as required by University in the exigency of service.

25.2 They shall only be compensated of the services rendered if any of the documentary requirements is satisfied. These documents include such as but not limited to, travel order, course module(s) approved for classroom utilization that is/are given to the students prior to the scheduled travel or screenshots of the activities done in the google classroom. The immediate supervisor may require other supporting documents, if necessary.
25.3 The supporting documents shall no longer be
attached to the Daily Time Record (DTR) of the faculty
concerned. The immediate supervisor, as accountable officer, shall verify and attest to the veracity and the authenticity of the proof(s) attached.
Section 26 The credit unit for lecture and laboratory classes is as
follows:

| A. Instruction | Credit Unit |
|---|---|
| A.1 Lecture and Laboratory Classes |  |
| For every hour lecture/week in the undergraduate level | 1.0 |
| For every 1 hour of laboratory work (for courses with laboratory) Note: A 3-unit course with laboratory will have corresponding WL with the formula below: WL = LeCUh+LaCUh Where: WL = Workload LeCU = Lecture credit unit LaCU Laboratory credit unit | 6 |

| h = corresponding hour Example: 3 units computer course taught in 2-hour lecture and 3-hour laboratory per week WL = LeCUh + LaCUh = 1.0 x 2 + 0.75 x 3 = 2.0 + 2.25 = 4.25 This does not apply to natural sciences laboratory courses, IT-related courses like networking, PC building, robotics; nursing, midwifery, maritime, hospitality and tourism, engineering and other programs with laboratory courses considered high-risk laboratory work exposure requiring close supervision of the instructor/professor. The program chair shall identify the high-risk laboratory courses concurred by the Associate Dean and the instructor/professor concerned. Illustration: 1 lab unit = 3 hours of lab work. The credit unit in hours is 3. 3 lab units = 9 hours of lab work. The credit unit in hours is 9. | 6 |
|---|---|
| Supervising instructor of student teaching (not to exceed credit unit of the course): - Student teaching of at least 10 students - Student teaching of less than 10 students | 4 |
| Field Study instructor | 3 |
| OJT/Practicum/Industry Immersion (regardless of lec/lab unit classification) | 0.3 per student but not exceed the credit unit of the course |
Section 27 Other quasi-judicial assignments such as coach, adviser,

coordinators trainer considered seasonal/occasional designations may still be given credit unit, but are financially compensated upon approval of the formal request and presentation of output/s subject to the availability of funds. If funds are not sufficient, they shall be compensated in the form of non-monetary renumeration. If COCs are maximized, they may be given recognition in other forms.
Section 28 An instructor with a WL of 30 (from doing research,

extension, IGP) but with a TL of only 24 units (teaching 8 courses of 3 units each) will not be given honoraria/ overload pay. It must be noted that researchers, extensionists, and IGP managers/workers are given incentives under separate incentive schemes.
Section 29 The maximum WL that will be considered for permanent
faculty is 30 units and for contractual faculty is 36 units.
Any WO in excess of that will not be given credit for COCs and treated as services to the university that may be given recognition in.
Section 30 A faculty member designated as Vice President/campus
Director satisfies the required WL of 24 units, hence he/she has the option to do activities supportive to any combination or all the mandated functions of the
University.
Section 31 For research functions/creative works, one research

study/creative work is equivalent to 3 units WL. A research study/creative work will be considered WL in a semester if it is to be implemented within a minimum of 3 months within said semester. Lower that that, it will be treated a fraction thereof (e.g. study for 2 months will be 1.2 WLL only as a result of 3/5 = 6 x 2 = 1.2). For team research/creative work, the equivalent WL units shall be divided based on the number of researchers/authors. For research studies/project/creative works funded by personal funds or sourced out from external funding agencies, these can be given due WL provided these had been properly reported to and recorder by CORDEX and a research/creative work contract had been executed between the researcher/author and the University stipulating among others that the result must be published
or patented. In application, a Professor required to have a mandatory research WL of 6 must have at least 2 research studies.
Section 32 On extension, short-term training/seminar shall be given

1-unit equivalent WL while long-term extension projects shall have an equivalent WL of 3 units. A short-term extension activity ranges from 6 months and below. A long-term extension activity is from seven (7) months and above. In case of no workload release, the extensionist(s) shall received cash incentive(s) subject to usual accounting and auditing rules and regulations shall be divided.
Section 33 Special courses offered upon request of students and
agreed to be handled by a faculty even without
remuneration must not be included as a WL/TL.
Section 34 In case of budget constraints, the teaching personnel who

render services in excess of the regular teaching load or outside of the regular office hours may be paid in a form of non-monetary remuneration pursuant to existing Civil Service rules and regulations, provided that he/she is informed and has given prior consent and approval as to
the mode of compensation. The Human Resource Management Office shall facilitate the preparation of the
Affidavit of Undertaking.
Section 35 Faculty members without designations handling courses

in specific programs, as provided in their respective programs, standards, and guidelines (PSG), shall be allowed not more than thirty-six (36) teaching hours per week and shall receive corresponding remuneration for the teaching overload.

35.1 Full-time faculty members may carry a combined RLE and classroom load of not more than thirty-six (36) hours per week which include consultation hours and other activities related to RLE instruction, research and
extension (CMO 15, s.2017).
35.2 The Dean and the Department Chair/Head may be
allowed to handle a maximum teaching load of not more than 12 hours a week provided that their primary duties and responsibilities are not neglected.
A full-time faculty may be allowed a maximum teaching load of 30 contact hours per week. However, a faculty
member with a documented Very Satisfactory teaching
performance may be allowed to handle additional six (6)
hours per week (CHED-MARINA Memorandum Circular No. 01, series of 2022).
Section 36 The computation oh honorarium based on the Prime
Hourly Teaching Rate (PHTR) for teaching overload in the
undergraduate program shall conform with Chapter 7,
Section 7.13 of the DBM Circular Letter No. 2007-6 (Manual on Position Compensation and Classification).
Section 37 The computation for overtime pay of the teaching overload
on a scheduled workday and on a rest day or scheduled day off, holiday, or special non-working day shall use the
formula as stipulated in the Joint Circular No. 02, s.2015 dated November 25, 2015 of the Civil Service Commission
and the Department of Budget and Management.
Section 38 Administrative personnel, regardless of employment

status, who wish to teach in the undergraduate program outside of the 8-hour work period shall be compensated on a fixed hourly rate based on the highest educational attainment and the expertise of the course(s) taught

Board Resolution No. 17-2005, Board pursuant to Resolution No. 44-205, Board Resolution No. 55- and 2016. The hourly rate as embodied in the aforementioned resolutions shall be subject to change should there be amendments in the succeeding year(s).
VII. Midyear Classes
Section 1 Faculty members assigned/requested to teach course(s)
in the undergraduate program during midyear break shall be allowed to handle a maximum of 2 courses/preparations or 9 teaching units.
Section 2 The compensation of honorarium based on the Prime
Hourly Teaching Rate (PHTR) for teaching overload in the
undergraduate program shall conform with Chapter 7,
Section 7,13 of the DBM Circular Letter No. 2007-6 (Manual on Position Compensation)
Section 3 Lecturers who wish to teach or are requested to teach in
the undergraduate program during midyear break shall be compensated on a fixed hourly rate based on the highest educational attainment and the expertise of the course(s)

Board Resolution No. 17-2005, Board taught pursuant to Resolution No. 44-2015, Board Resolution No. 55- and 2016. The hourly rate as embodied in the aforementioned resolutions shall be subject to change should there be amendments in the succeeding year(s).
VIII. Outside Teaching Load
Section 1 Outside teaching load refers to academic instruction
rendered in other colleges or universities. The schedule for outside teaching should be outside the regular official and overload teaching time.
Section 2 Faculty members who wish to teach outside should secure
the permission and approval from the program chair,
Associate Dean, Dean, Campus Director, Vice President for Academic Affairs, and the University President, respectively.
Section 3 An outside teaching load of not more than nine (9) units
for lecture and five (5) units for laboratory may be allowed to a faculty member.
Section 4 Faculty on study leave with pay or on scholarship shall not
be allowed to render outside teaching loads.
IX. Separability Clause
Section 1 If, for any reason, any part of provision of this policy and
guideline is declared invalid or unconstitutional, the remaining parts or provisions not affected thereby shall remain in full force and effect.
X. Effectivity
Section 1 These Policies on Faculty Workload approved by the
JRMSU Board of Regents per Resolution No. 145-2024
dated September 24, 2024 shall take effect this Academic
